title: The Poet�s New Year�s Gift. To Mrs. (Afterwards Lady) Throckmorton.
content:
Maria! I have every good
    For thee wish�d many a time,
    Both sad, and in a cheerful mood,
    But never yet in rhyme.


    To wish thee fairer is no need,
    More prudent, or more sprightly,
    Or more ingenious, or more freed
    From temper flaws unsightly.


    What favour then not yet possess�d
    Can I for thee require,
    In wedded love already blest,
    To thy whole heart�s desire?


    None here is happy but in part;
    Full bliss is bliss divine;
    There dwells some wish in every heart,
    And doubtless one in thine.


    That wish on some fair future day,
    Which fate shall brightly gild
    (�Tis blameless, be it what it may),
    I wish it all fulfill�d.
meaning:
William Cowper's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Poet�s New Year�s Gift. To Mrs. (Afterwards Lady) Throckmorton."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
